latest.title: Cessation of Crypto Treasury Management Strategy
latest.content:
Today 07:30
Anemoi International Ltd (AMOI) Cessation of Crypto Treasury Management Strategy
26-Feb-2026 / 07:30 GMT/BST
Anemoi International Ltd
Anemoi International Ltd
(Reuters: AMOI.L, Bloomberg: AMOI:LN)
("Anemoi" or the "Company")
Cessation of Crypto Treasury Management Strategy
Further to the Company’s announcement dated 24 November 2025 in respect of the sale of crypto related investments, the Board confirms it has no further exposure to Crypto currencies, and that given the Company’s previously announced RTO transaction, it has ceased pursuing a crypto treasury management strategy and has no intention of re-initiating any crypto treasury strategy.
Commenting,
Duncan Soukup said “Our foray into Crypto was highly lucrative but now that the Company has embarked on an RTO strategy, the Company’s Board has decided to cease all Crypto Treasury activities.”
END
Anemoi International Ltd
www.anemoi-international.com
Enquiries
enquiries@anemoi-international.com

latest.title: Admission to Trading on the American OTCQB Market
latest.content:
4 Feb 2026 07:30
Anemoi International Ltd (AMOI) Admission to Trading on the American OTCQB Market
04-Feb-2026 / 07:30 GMT/BST
Anemoi International Ltd
Anemoi International Ltd
(Reuters: AMOI.L, Bloomberg: AMOI:LN)
("Anemoi" or the "Company")
Admission to Trading on the American OTCQB Market
Anemoi International Ltd (
XLON:AMOI / OTCQB:AMOIF
), a London Stock Exchange Main Market listed technology company, is pleased to announce that its shares ("Ordinary Shares") have been approved to trade on the OTCQB Venture Market ("OTCQB") in the United States and will commence trading on the OTCQB market at the open today, under the symbol "AMOIF".
This will not affect trading of the Company's Ordinary Shares on the London Stock Exchange, which will continue under the symbol "AMOI", and no new Ordinary Shares have been issued through this process. The Company will continue to make all announcements and disclosures to the London Stock Exchange as normal and is not subject to any Sarbanes-Oxley or U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission reporting requirements.
The Company believes that trading on the OTCQB will enable US based investors to purchase and hold shares of the Company, which they would otherwise be unable to hold in certain US brokerage accounts thereby adding to the liquidity of the stock. A further compelling reason for the OTC listing is the Company’s recent announcement (
AMOI:Trasna
) in respect of the proposed $150 million acquisition of the Trasna Group of Companies.
About Trasna:
https://www.trasna.io/
Trasna is a global technology leader specialising in semiconductor and mobile Internet of Things (“IoT”) solutions. With more than 600 employees and 200 clients worldwide, it combines expertise in semiconductors, secure data management, edge computing, AI, and blockchain to create innovative, end-to-end solutions - from chip design and SIM manufacturing to over-the-air subscription and device management.
Trasna provides end-to-end cellular IoT solutions with a fully integrated hardware and software stack, providing complete IoT lifecycle management solutions. Trasna’s aim is the simplification of mass IoT, focusing on security, efficiency, and innovation. Trasna has been hailed as one of the most complete, innovative, and fastest-to-deploy eSIM solution providers in the World.
Duncan Soukup, Chairman, commented:
“We are excited by the opportunities that access to US investors through OTC Markets offers. AMOI’s RTO transaction with Trasna will be transformational for both companies, and the OTC Listing can be seen as Trasna’s first step into the US capital markets. Trasna’s current sales in the US are less than 10%, allowing for substantial future growth; with direct access to US investors now secured, Trasna can also look forward to potential interest from Wall Street analysts and US Investment Banks, which should help create a solid platform to support the Company’s Global growth ambitions.”
“I would like to extend my personal thanks to Jonathan Dickson and the entire OTC Markets Team for their professional support during the application and onboarding process. it has been a great pleasure working with you all. Thank you and we look forward to the future on OTC Markets confident in the knowledge that we are in good hands”
END
Anemoi International Ltd
www.anemoi-international.com
Enquiries
enquiries@anemoi-international.com

Anemoi International Ltd (AMOI) Anemoi International Ltd: Board Changes

Anemoi International Ltd
Anemoi International Ltd
(Reuters: AMOI.L, Bloomberg: AMOI:LN)
("Anemoi" or the "Company")
Board Changes
The Company announces that, with immediate effect, Richard Emanuel has stepped down from the Board of Directors to pursue other opportunities.
The Board would like to wish Richard every success in his future endeavours.

company_30s.what_they_do: Anemoi International Ltd builds and sells blockchain‑based crypto‑treasury and compliance tools for institutional investors.  Its products include a KYC/AML platform (ID4) and a suite of smart‑contract‑based treasury services that let firms hold, move and audit digital assets securely.
company_30s.why_they_matter: The company is positioned at the intersection of two fast‑growing markets: institutional crypto adoption and regulated compliance.  As banks and asset managers seek to add crypto to their portfolios, they need audited, compliant custody solutions – a niche that Anemoi claims to serve with its ID4 and treasury platform.  The partnership with Azilen and the recent acquisition of Trasna give it new technology and a broader client base.
company_30s.current_state: Anemoi is in a pivot phase.  It has stopped its own crypto‑treasury strategy, is focusing on selling its compliance platform, and has just moved to the OTCQB market to raise capital.  The float is tiny, so price swings are large, and the stock is trading far below its historic peak.

rns_narrative: The company has shifted from a self‑run treasury service to a B2B compliance provider, signing a reseller deal with its KYC/AML subsidiary ID4 and forming a strategic partnership with Azilen.  The acquisition of Trasna expands its product offering and adds a new revenue stream.

Meanwhile, Anemoi has moved to the OTCQB market, signalling a new funding round and an attempt to broaden its investor base.  The cessation of its own treasury strategy shows a focus on core strengths and a move away from high‑risk crypto operations.
Overall, the narrative is one of restructuring: cutting high‑cost services, adding a partnership that can generate recurring revenue, and seeking fresh capital through a secondary listing.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Quiet optimism – traders are waiting for a clear sign that the pivot will pay off.
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: Bullish voices point to the Trasna acquisition and the ID4 reseller deal as evidence that the company can generate stable revenue once the new products launch.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Bears warn that the company has no current revenue, a very small float, and is still under regulatory scrutiny after the suspension issue.  They fear the new listing may not bring the needed capital.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: Sentiment has moved from cautious to mildly hopeful after the acquisition news, but overall engagement remains low.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends for the ticker is flat – there is no current search interest, giving the stock a surprise‑potential advantage when a catalyst hits.

near_term_catalysts: A formal announcement of revenue or a signed contract from a new client using the ID4 platform.
The official listing on the OTCQB and a corresponding volume spike as new investors buy.
Completion of the Trasna integration – any press release about combined product offerings or early beta feedback.
expect_next.watch_for: Look for a volume surge of at least 200% above the 20‑day average and a price break above the 2‑p IPO level.  A clear support at the 2‑p mark that holds for at least three days would confirm a bottom.
expect_next.timing_regime: The price appears to be in the late‑stage of a capitulation cycle – the depth of the crash is significant and the float is very small.  The APEX score suggests a tight squeeze; if the stock bounces off the 2‑p support, it could trigger a broader rally.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: The stock is deeply discounted, and the recent acquisition plus partnership signal new revenue streams.  If the price recovers to the 2‑p support, that level represents roughly a 70‑80% bounce from the recent low – a return that aligns with the historic 39–56% average for green and orange signals.  The asymmetry is clear: a small downside risk (down to zero) versus a large upside if the mean reversion plays out.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: The company has no current revenue and a very small float, which could lead to extreme volatility.  Regulatory uncertainty from the past suspension and the risk that the OTCQB listing fails to raise sufficient capital could keep the price stuck below the 2‑p mark.  A worst‑case scenario is a continued decline to zero if the pivot fails.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential loss: up to 100% of the invested capital if the price collapses to zero.  Potential gain: a 70–80% upside if the price rebounds to 2‑p.  The reward is roughly 1.5–2 times the risk, a classic contrarian asymmetry.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter after a confirmed break above the 2‑p support with volume 200%+ of the 20‑day average.  Size the position to risk no more than 5% of your portfolio per trade.  Place a stop just below the 2‑p support (e.g., 1.8‑p) to protect against a false break.
playbook.if_watching: Hold until a clear RNS about new client contracts or the official OTCQB listing is released.  If the stock stays below 2‑p for 30 days with flat volume, consider walking away.
playbook.if_passing: No terminal risks have surfaced yet; the only genuine risk is a failure to raise capital on the OTCQB.  If a delisting notice or bankruptcy filing appears, exit immediately.

ai_insights.bottom_line: Anemoi’s deep crash and the recent acquisition/partnership give a contrarian bullish case, but only if the stock can bounce off the 2‑p support and the new business model starts generating revenue.
